ginger_tea.name: Ginger Tea
ginger_tea.prep: Steep 1 tsp fresh grated ginger in a cup of hot water for 10 minutes; add honey or lemon to taste.
ginger_tea.traditional_use: Classical digestive stimulant (deepana-pachana) used for kapha-vata imbalances and nausea.
ginger_tea.modern_evidence: Multiple RCTs and a Cochrane review support ginger for nausea, including pregnancy-related nausea at culinary doses, and for mild dyspepsia.
cautions: Avoid high supplemental doses alongside anticoagulant medication.

nutmeg_warm_milk.name: Warm Nutmeg Milk
nutmeg_warm_milk.prep: A pinch of nutmeg powder stirred into a cup of warm milk before bed.
nutmeg_warm_milk.modern_evidence: Limited small studies suggest mild sedative effect; evidence is weak.
cautions: Avoid large doses — nutmeg is toxic in excess.

triphala.name: Triphala
triphala.prep: 1/2 tsp Triphala powder dissolved in warm water, taken at bedtime.
triphala.traditional_use: Classical rasayana formula for digestion and elimination, combining amalaki, bibhitaki, and haritaki.
triphala.modern_evidence: Small clinical trials show mild laxative and prebiotic effects.

honey_lemon.name: Honey Lemon Warm Water
honey_lemon.prep: 1 tsp raw honey with fresh lemon juice stirred into warm (not hot) water.
honey_lemon.traditional_use: Traditional soothing remedy for kapha-related cough and throat irritation.
honey_lemon.modern_evidence: A Cochrane review supports honey for reducing acute cough frequency and severity.
cautions: Never give to infants under 12 months — risk of infant botulism.

tulsi_tea.name: Tulsi (Holy Basil) Tea
tulsi_tea.prep: Steep 5-6 fresh or dried tulsi leaves in hot water for 8-10 minutes.
tulsi_tea.traditional_use: Widely used adaptogen and respiratory support herb in Ayurveda.
tulsi_tea.modern_evidence: Small trials suggest immunomodulatory and mild antitussive effects.
cautions: May have mild blood-sugar-lowering effect; monitor if diabetic.

cumin_coriander_fennel_tea.name: CCF Tea (Cumin-Coriander-Fennel)
cumin_coriander_fennel_tea.prep: Steep 1/4 tsp each of cumin, coriander, and fennel seeds in hot water for 10 minutes.
cumin_coriander_fennel_tea.traditional_use: Classic tridoshic digestive tea considered gentle enough for daily use.
cumin_coriander_fennel_tea.modern_evidence: Component herbs individually show mild carminative effects in small studies.
cautions: Generally well tolerated, including in pregnancy at culinary doses.
